title: The rise of HELOCs: What it means for originators in today’s market

sources.title: The rise of HELOCs: What it means for originators in today’s market
sources.excerpt: As interest rates remain elevated compared to historic, pandemic-era lows, many homeowners are rethinking how to access cash without giving up the favorable rates they locked in just a few years ago. For originators, that shift is creating a clear opportunity. Demand for home equity lines of credit (HELOCs) is gaining momentum, as borrowers seek flexible ways to tap into home equity while keeping their existing first mortgages in place.

title: Chain reaction: A framework for America’s housing and retirement crises
summary: The American Dream has a math problem. Millions can't afford homes. Those who can, can't find them. And many who already own don't have enough saved to retire. It's gridlock, compounding and dangerou

sources.title: Chain reaction: A framework for America’s housing and retirement crises
sources.excerpt: The American Dream has a math problem. Millions can't afford homes. Those who can, can't find them. And many who already own don't have enough saved to retire. It's gridlock, compounding and dangerous enough to stifle homeownership as a pathway to stability, wealth creation, and retirement longevity. There's been no shortage of possible fixes suggested, but they’ve largely existed in silos depending on who’s proposing them. Nothing systemic has materialized.
